conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I'd like to gather some more information about Alex to assess your eligibility.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18, I need to understand his current situation as eligibility rules can vary for children over 16.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he studying full-time at school, college, or on an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you, or is he living elsewhere (such as in care, in hospital for an extended period, or with someone else)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand a bit about your own circumstances.

**Are you responsible for Alex?** In other words, do you provide for his care and support (such as housing, food, and general upkeep)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for him - I provide his housing, food, and everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 18 and in approved full-time education (doing A-levels at college)
✓ You can claim Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training
✓ Alex lives with you and you're responsible for his care
✓ You live in the UK
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. You can continue to claim until he turns 20 or leaves approved education or training, whichever comes first.

If you haven't already applied, you can make a claim through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and still in approved full-time education (doing A-levels at college). According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Alex lives at home with the claimant, who is responsible for his care and provides housing, food, and general support. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age extension criteria as he is under 20 and in approved full-time education (A-levels). The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.
